title: linum kurdicum
intro: Linum kurdicum is a flowering plant species, also known as Kurdish flax or Kurdish mille, that is native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and Iran. Traditionally, Linum kurdicum has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support digestive health. The herbal preparations of Linum kurdicum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
